annotations.label: The A2 root system (six vectors in R²)

anchor.snippet: These six vectors satisfy the following definition, and therefore they form a root system

annotations.note: The general root-system definition exists and CartanMatrix.A 2 is available, but the explicit six-vector A₂ root system is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Root system

anchor.snippet: is a finite set of non-zero vectors (called roots ) that satisfy the following conditions

annotations.note: RootPairing with the IsRootSystem class formalizes the spanning + reflection-stability axioms (more general: any commutative ring, integrality factored out as IsCrystallographic).

annotations.label: Equivalent forms of conditions 3 and 4

anchor.snippet: Equivalent ways of writing conditions 3 and 4, respectively, are as follows

annotations.note: Reflection-stability is captured by mapsTo_reflection_root and integrality by pairingIn/IsCrystallographic, but the explicit equivalences are not stated as such.

annotations.label: Crystallographic root system

anchor.snippet: a root system that also satisfies the integrality condition is known as a crystallographic root system

annotations.note: IsCrystallographic is defined as IsValuedIn ℤ, i.e. every root-coroot pairing is an integer.

annotations.label: Reduced root system

anchor.snippet: then they call root systems satisfying condition 2 reduced

annotations.note: IsReduced states that linearly dependent roots differ only by a sign.

annotations.label: Integrality equivalent to reflection differing by integer multiple

anchor.snippet: the integrality condition is equivalent to stating that

annotations.note: reflection_apply_root' shows s_α(β) = β - (pairingIn ℤ β α)•α, expressing the reflection as an integer-multiple shift, but the equivalence with integrality is not isolated as a lemma.

anchors.snippet: the integrality condition is equivalent to stating that

annotations.label: The pairing operator is not an inner product

anchor.snippet: It is not necessarily symmetric and is linear only in the first argument

annotations.note: The pairing is built from linear functionals root'/coroot' (linear in the first argument), but the assertion of non-symmetry / not-an-inner-product is not a stated theorem.

anchors.snippet: It is not necessarily symmetric and is linear only in the first argument

annotations.label: Rank of a root system

anchor.snippet: The rank of a root system Φ is the dimension of E

annotations.note: There is no dedicated `rank` definition, but finrank of the (root)span/ambient module captures the dimension.

annotations.label: Irreducible root system

anchor.snippet: A root system which does not arise from such a combination

annotations.note: IsIrreducible encodes the absence of proper reflection-invariant submodules, equivalent to non-decomposability.

annotations.label: Isomorphic root systems

anchor.snippet: are called isomorphic if there is an invertible linear transformation

annotations.note: RootPairing.Equiv is a linear isomorphism of weight spaces compatible with roots/coroots.

annotations.label: Root lattice

anchor.snippet: The root lattice of a root system Φ is the Z -submodule of E generated by Φ

annotations.note: rootSpan ℤ = span ℤ (range P.root) is exactly the root lattice (rootSpan is the general S-span of roots).

annotations.label: Weyl group of a root system

anchor.snippet: The group of isometries of E generated by reflections through hyperplanes associated to the roots of Φ

annotations.note: weylGroup is the subgroup of automorphisms generated by the reflections.

annotations.label: The Weyl group is finite

anchor.snippet: As it acts faithfully on the finite set Φ, the Weyl group is always finite

annotations.note: No Finite/Fintype instance for weylGroup is present; faithfulness of the index representation is even listed as a TODO.

annotations.label: The unique rank-one root system A1

anchor.snippet: There is only one root system of rank 1, consisting of two nonzero vectors

annotations.note: The rank-one classification/uniqueness is not formalized.

annotations.label: The four rank-two root systems

anchor.snippet: In rank 2 there are four possibilities

annotations.note: No enumeration/classification of rank-two root systems exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: A root system is not determined by its lattice

anchor.snippet: Note that a root system is not determined by the lattice that it generates

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Intersection with a subspace is a root system

anchor.snippet: then Ψ is a root system in S

annotations.note: There is no result constructing a root system from the intersection of Φ with a subspace.

annotations.label: Possible angles between two roots

anchor.snippet: two such roots must meet at an angle of 0, 30, 45, 60, 90, 120, 135, 150, or 180 degrees

annotations.note: The integer pairing-pairs are restricted to the finite set corresponding to these angles, but angles themselves are not computed.

annotations.label: Root of a semisimple Lie algebra relative to a Cartan subalgebra

anchor.section: Root systems arising from semisimple Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: we can construct a root system as follows

annotations.note: Roots are the nonzero weights (Weight K H L), indexed by H.root, used to build rootSystem.

anchors.section: Root systems arising from semisimple Lie algebras
anchors.snippet: we can construct a root system as follows

annotations.label: Roots of a semisimple Lie algebra form a root system

anchor.section: Root systems arising from semisimple Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: there is an inner product for which the set of roots forms a root system

annotations.note: rootSystem with its IsRootSystem instance proves the roots of a finite splitting semisimple (Killing) Lie algebra form a root system.

anchors.section: Root systems arising from semisimple Lie algebras
anchors.snippet: there is an inner product for which the set of roots forms a root system

annotations.label: Cosine of angle between roots is constrained

anchor.section: Elementary consequences of the root system axioms
anchor.snippet: The cosine of the angle between two roots is constrained to be one-half of the square root of a positive integer

annotations.note: The Coxeter weight (=4cos²θ) is shown to be a bounded nonnegative integer, giving the constraint, but the cosine itself is not formalized.

anchors.section: Elementary consequences of the root system axioms
anchors.snippet: The cosine of the angle between two roots is constrained to be one-half of the square root of a positive integer

annotations.label: Possible angle and length-ratio pairs

anchor.section: Elementary consequences of the root system axioms
anchor.snippet: In summary, here are the only possibilities for each pair of roots

annotations.note: The classification of (⟨α,β⟩,⟨β,α⟩) pairs into the finite admissible set is exactly the angle/length-ratio table.

annotations.label: Set of positive roots

anchor.snippet: we can always choose (in many ways) a set of positive roots

annotations.note: Positivity relative to a base is defined via Base.IsPos (positive height); the separating-functional formulation is via baseOf.

annotations.label: Negative roots

anchor.snippet: are called negative roots

annotations.note: Negative roots are the complement of IsPos (IsPos.neg_iff_not), but there is no separate `IsNeg` definition.

annotations.label: Every set of positive roots arises from a hyperplane

anchor.snippet: every set of positive roots arises in this way

annotations.note: Not formalized; Mathlib only constructs positive systems from a functional, not the converse.

annotations.label: Simple root (base)

anchor.snippet: is called a simple root (also fundamental root ) if it cannot be written as the sum of two elements

annotations.note: RootPairing.Base bundles the support of simple roots; existence is RootPairing.nonempty_base.

annotations.label: Simple roots form a basis with special properties

anchor.snippet: with the following additional special properties

annotations.note: A base yields a basis (toWeightBasis) and exists_root_eq_sum_int shows each root is an integer combination with uniform sign.

annotations.label: Sets of positive roots differ by the Weyl group

anchor.snippet: any two sets of positive roots differ by the action of the Weyl group

annotations.note: The simply-transitive Weyl-group action on bases is noted in docs but not formalized.

annotations.label: Coroot

anchor.snippet: the coroot α ∨ of a root α is defined by

annotations.note: Coroots are part of the RootPairing data, with root_coroot_two giving ⟨α,α∨⟩=2.

anchors.snippet: the coroot α ∨ of a root α is defined by

annotations.label: Dual root system

anchor.snippet: The set of coroots also forms a root system Φ ∨ in E , called the dual root system

annotations.note: P.flip swaps roots and coroots and is again a root pairing/system.

anchors.snippet: The set of coroots also forms a root system Φ ∨ in E , called the dual root system

annotations.label: Double coroot equals the root

anchor.snippet: By direct calculation, α ∨∨ = α

annotations.note: flip_flip : P.flip.flip = P, and P.flip.coroot = P.root, giving α∨∨ = α.

annotations.label: Dual of simple roots are simple roots of the dual

anchor.snippet: then Δ ∨ is a set of simple roots for Φ ∨

annotations.note: Base.flip gives a base of P.flip with the same support, i.e. the coroots of Δ form a base of Φ∨.

annotations.label: Self-dual root systems

anchor.snippet: are all self-dual, meaning that the dual root system is isomorphic to the original root system

annotations.note: No self-duality results for specific types are formalized.

annotations.label: Integral element

anchor.snippet: if its inner product with each coroot is an integer

annotations.note: No notion of integral element / weight lattice element is defined.

anchors.snippet: if its inner product with each coroot is an integer

annotations.label: Weight lattice

anchor.snippet: The set of integral elements is called the weight lattice associated to the given root system

annotations.note: The weight lattice is not defined in Mathlib's root-system development.

annotations.label: Roots are integral elements

anchor.snippet: guarantees that the roots themselves are integral elements

annotations.note: The integrality of root-coroot pairings (roots being integral) is exactly IsCrystallographic, but the weight-lattice framing is absent.

annotations.label: Irreducible root system (via partition)

anchor.section: Classification of root systems by Dynkin diagrams
anchor.snippet: A root system is irreducible if it cannot be partitioned into the union of two proper subsets

annotations.note: IsIrreducible (no proper invariant submodule) is the formalized notion, equivalent to non-partitionability.

annotations.label: Irreducible root systems correspond to Dynkin diagrams

anchor.section: Classification of root systems by Dynkin diagrams
anchor.snippet: Irreducible root systems correspond to certain graphs

annotations.note: The classification of irreducible root systems by Dynkin diagrams is not formalized.

annotations.label: Constructing the Dynkin diagram from simple roots

anchor.snippet: The vertices of the associated Dynkin diagram correspond to the roots in Δ

annotations.note: The Cartan matrix relative to a base is defined (Base.cartanMatrix), but the Dynkin diagram graph itself is not built from it.

annotations.label: Weyl group acts transitively on choices of simple roots

anchor.snippet: the Weyl group acts transitively on such choices

annotations.note: Transitivity of the Weyl group on bases is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dynkin diagram is independent of choice of simple roots

anchor.snippet: the Dynkin diagram is independent of the choice of simple roots

annotations.note: No result that the Cartan matrix/Dynkin diagram is independent of the base choice.

annotations.label: Irreducible iff connected Dynkin diagram

anchor.snippet: A root systems is irreducible if and only if its Dynkin diagram is connected

annotations.note: induction_on_cartanMatrix is a connectedness characterisation of the Dynkin diagram for irreducible pairings, but the full iff is not stated.

annotations.label: Dynkin diagram of the dual reverses arrows

anchor.snippet: by keeping all the same vertices and edges, but reversing the directions of all arrows

annotations.note: pairing_flip shows the dual's pairing is the transpose (Cartan matrix transposed = reversed arrows), but no Dynkin-diagram statement exists.

annotations.label: Weyl chamber

anchor.section: Weyl chambers and the Weyl group
anchor.snippet: each connected component is called a Weyl chamber

annotations.note: Weyl chambers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental Weyl chamber

anchor.section: Weyl chambers and the Weyl group
anchor.snippet: we may define the fundamental Weyl chamber associated to Δ

annotations.note: Not formalized (no Weyl chambers).

annotations.label: Weyl group permutes the Weyl chambers

anchor.section: Weyl chambers and the Weyl group
anchor.snippet: each Weyl group element permutes the Weyl chambers

annotations.note: Not formalized (no Weyl chambers).

annotations.label: Weyl group acts freely and transitively on Weyl chambers

anchor.section: Weyl chambers and the Weyl group
anchor.snippet: The Weyl group acts freely and transitively on the Weyl chambers

annotations.note: Not formalized (no Weyl chambers).

annotations.label: Each Weyl orbit meets a chamber closure in one point

anchor.section: Weyl chambers and the Weyl group
anchor.snippet: contains exactly one point in the closure

annotations.note: Not formalized (no Weyl chambers).

annotations.label: Irreducible root systems classify Lie-theoretic objects

anchor.snippet: Irreducible root systems classify a number of related objects in Lie theory

annotations.note: The classification correspondence with Lie-theoretic objects is not formalized.

annotations.label: Roots are weights of the adjoint representation

anchor.snippet: the roots are non-zero weights of the adjoint representation

annotations.note: Roots are defined as nonzero weights (Weight K H L) of H acting on L (adjoint), so this is definitional rather than a stated theorem.

annotations.label: Semisimple Lie algebra simple iff root system irreducible

anchor.snippet: a semisimple Lie algebra is simple if and only if the associated root system is irreducible

annotations.note: isSimple_iff_isIrreducible proves a Killing Lie algebra is simple iff its root system is irreducible.

annotations.label: Cartan subalgebras give isomorphic root systems

anchor.snippet: any two Cartan subalgebras give isomorphic root systems

annotations.note: Independence of the root system from the choice of Cartan subalgebra is not formalized.

annotations.label: Root system determines the Lie algebra up to isomorphism

anchor.snippet: the root system determines the Lie algebra up to isomorphism

annotations.note: No uniqueness theorem reconstructing the Lie algebra from its root system is formalized.

annotations.label: Each irreducible root system has an associated simple Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: there is an associated simple Lie algebra

annotations.note: Geck's construction builds a (semisimple) Lie algebra from a reduced crystallographic root system with base, giving the existence direction, but full simple-Lie-algebra existence/classification is not assembled.

annotations.label: Four classical families and five exceptional cases

anchor.section: Properties of the irreducible root systems
anchor.snippet: There are four infinite families

annotations.note: Cartan matrices for the families A,B,C,D and exceptionals E₆,E₇,E₈,F₄,G₂ are defined, but the completeness/classification theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: At most two root lengths

anchor.section: Properties of the irreducible root systems
anchor.snippet: there can be at most two values for the length

annotations.note: exists_apply_eq_or proves a reduced irreducible finite crystallographic root system has at most two root lengths.

annotations.label: Simply laced root system

anchor.section: Properties of the irreducible root systems
anchor.snippet: the root system is said to be simply laced

annotations.note: IsSimplyLaced is defined for Cartan matrices (off-diagonal 0 or -1), but not as a property of an abstract root pairing.

annotations.label: Equal-length roots lie in same Weyl orbit

anchor.section: Properties of the irreducible root systems
anchor.snippet: Any two roots of the same length lie in the same orbit of the Weyl group

annotations.note: No result placing equal-length roots in a common Weyl orbit is formalized.

annotations.label: The Aₙ root system

anchor.snippet: Let E be the subspace of R n +1 for which the coordinates sum to 0

annotations.note: Only the type-A Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit Aₙ root system in R^{n+1} is not constructed.

annotations.label: Aₙ root lattice

anchor.snippet: is most easily described as the set of integer vectors in R n +1 whose components sum to zero

annotations.note: The Aₙ root lattice description is not formalized.

annotations.label: The Bₙ root system

anchor.snippet: let Φ consist of all integer vectors in E of length 1 or √ 2

annotations.note: Only the type-B Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit Bₙ root system is not constructed.

annotations.label: Bₙ root lattice is all integer vectors

anchor.snippet: The B n root lattice—that is, the lattice generated by the B n roots—consists of all integer vectors

annotations.note: The Bₙ root lattice description is not formalized.

annotations.label: B1 is isomorphic to A1

anchor.snippet: B 1 is isomorphic to A 1 via scaling by √ 2

annotations.note: B_one : B 1 = A 1 gives the coincidence at the Cartan-matrix level, not a root-system isomorphism.

annotations.label: The Cₙ root system

anchor.snippet: let Φ consist of all integer vectors in E of length √ 2 together with all vectors of the form 2 λ

annotations.note: Only the type-C Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit Cₙ root system is not constructed.

annotations.label: Cₙ root lattice

anchor.snippet: The C n root lattice—that is, the lattice generated by the C n roots—consists of all integer vectors whose components sum to an even integer

annotations.note: The Cₙ root lattice description is not formalized.

annotations.label: C2 is isomorphic to B2

anchor.snippet: C 2 is isomorphic to B 2 via scaling by √ 2 and a 45 degree rotation

annotations.note: C_transpose : (C n).transpose = B n encodes B/C duality at the Cartan-matrix level, not the C₂≅B₂ root-system isomorphism.

annotations.label: The Dₙ root system

anchor.snippet: The total number of roots is 2 n ( n − 1)

annotations.note: Only the type-D Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit Dₙ root system and root count are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dₙ root lattice equals Cₙ root lattice

anchor.snippet: This is the same as the C n root lattice

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: D3 coincides with A3

anchor.snippet: D 3 coincides with A 3

annotations.note: D_three' : (D 3).reindex ... = A 3 gives the coincidence at the Cartan-matrix level.

annotations.label: D4 has triality symmetry

anchor.snippet: D 4 has additional symmetry called triality

annotations.note: Triality of D₄ is not formalized.

annotations.label: The E8 root system

anchor.snippet: The E 8 root system is any set of vectors in R 8 that is congruent to the following set

annotations.note: Only the E₈ Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit E₈ root system in R⁸ is not constructed.

anchors.snippet: The E 8 root system is any set of vectors in R 8 that is congruent to the following set

annotations.label: E8 has 240 roots

anchor.snippet: The root system has 240 roots

annotations.note: The E₈ root count is not formalized.

annotations.label: The E7 root system

anchor.snippet: is the set of vectors in E 8 that are perpendicular to a fixed root in E 8

annotations.note: Only the E₇ Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit E₇ root system is not constructed.

annotations.label: The E6 root system

anchor.snippet: is the subsystem of E 8 perpendicular to two suitably chosen roots of E 8

annotations.note: Only the E₆ Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit E₆ root system is not constructed.

annotations.label: The lattices Γ8 and Γ'8 are isomorphic

anchor.snippet: The lattices Γ 8 and Γ' 8 are isomorphic

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: The F4 root system

anchor.snippet: let Φ denote the set of vectors α of length 1 or √ 2 such that the coordinates of 2α are all integers

annotations.note: Only the F₄ Cartan matrix is defined; the explicit F₄ root system is not constructed.

annotations.label: F4 root lattice is the Hurwitz quaternions

anchor.snippet: This lattice is isomorphic to the lattice of Hurwitz quaternions

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: The G2 root system

anchor.snippet: The root system G 2 has 12 roots, which form the vertices of a hexagram

annotations.note: IsG2/EmbeddedG2 abstractly characterise the 𝔤₂ root system and card_index_eq_twelve proves it has 12 roots, but the explicit hexagram construction is not given.

annotations.label: G2 root lattice equals A2 root lattice

anchor.snippet: is the same as the A 2 root lattice

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: The root poset ordering

anchor.snippet: The set of positive roots is naturally ordered by saying that

annotations.note: The height function (monotone for this order) is defined, but the root poset ordering itself is not introduced.

annotations.label: Degrees of fundamental invariants from the root poset

anchor.snippet: one can determine the degrees of the fundamental invariants of the corresponding Weyl group from this poset

annotations.note: Not formalized.
